hook.text: Your company wants to sell to banks. First, you need SOC 2 certification. Welcome to cloud compliance.

buildup.text: Compliance frameworks — SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA — define security standards your cloud setup must meet.

discovery.text: Cloud providers are pre-certified for many frameworks. But that only covers their part of shared responsibility.

twist.text: Being compliant doesn't mean being secure. Compliance is a minimum bar, not a guarantee against breaches.

climax.text: Smart companies use compliance as a starting point, then build security beyond what the framework requires.

punchline.text: Compliance opens doors. Real security keeps them safe.

quiz.question: Why doesn't compliance guarantee security?
options: Compliance is a minimum standard, not a comprehensive defense against all threats
Compliance frameworks are never updated for new threats
Cloud providers handle all compliance so customers don't need to worry
